info.name: Crocodilian
info.description: A playable crocodilian race for Aurora Builder, adapted from the D&D Wiki Crocodilian race concept.
info.author: D&D Wiki / Homebrew Adaptation

description.p: Crocodilians are powerful reptilian humanoids with thick scales, crushing jaws, muscular tails, and an instinctive connection to rivers, swamps, and deep water. They are often broad, heavy, and intimidating, built for ambush and endurance rather than grace.
Your crocodilian character has the following racial traits.

Ability Score Increase.
Your Constitution score increases by 2, and your Wisdom score increases by 1.

Age.
Crocodilians mature around the same age as lizardfolk and rarely live longer than 60 years.

Alignment.
Crocodilians tend toward neutrality. They understand the world through survival, territory, strength, and patience.

Size.
Crocodilians are bulky and powerful, often standing taller and weighing more than most humans. Your size is Medium.

Speed.
Your base walking speed is 30 feet, and you have a swimming speed of 30 feet.

Languages.
You can speak, read, and write Common and Draconic.

Hold Breath.
You can hold your breath for up to 15 minutes at a time.

Hunter's Lore.
You gain proficiency with two of the following skills of your choice: Animal Handling, Nature, Perception, Stealth, and Survival.

Crushing Bite.
Your powerful jaws are natural weapons, which you can use to make unarmed strikes. If you hit with your bite, you deal piercing damage equal to 1d8 + your Strength modifier instead of the bludgeoning damage normal for an unarmed strike.

Tail Attack.
Your tail is a natural weapon, which you can use to make unarmed strikes. If you hit with your tail, you deal bludgeoning damage equal to 1d6 + your Strength modifier.

Powerful Build.
You count as one size larger when determining your carrying capacity and the weight you can push, drag, or lift.

Grasping Jaws.
Immediately after hitting a creature with your bite attack, you can make a grapple attempt against that creature with advantage without using an action.

setters.set: Brak, Ghar, Kresh, Rusk, Ssar, Thok
Ashka, Brina, Kessa, Nira, Satha, Veshka

description.p: You can hold your breath for up to 15 minutes at a time.

sheet.description: You can hold your breath for up to 15 minutes at a time.

description.p: You gain proficiency with two of the following skills of your choice: Animal Handling, Nature, Perception, Stealth, and Survival.

sheet.description: You gain proficiency with two of the following skills: Animal Handling, Nature, Perception, Stealth, and Survival.

description.p: Your powerful jaws are natural weapons, which you can use to make unarmed strikes. If you hit with your bite, you deal piercing damage equal to 1d8 + your Strength modifier instead of the bludgeoning damage normal for an unarmed strike.

sheet.description: Your bite is a natural weapon. On hit, it deals 1d8 + {{strength:modifier}} piercing damage.

description.p: Your tail is a natural weapon, which you can use to make unarmed strikes. If you hit with your tail, you deal bludgeoning damage equal to 1d6 + your Strength modifier.

sheet.description: Your tail is a natural weapon. On hit, it deals 1d6 + {{strength:modifier}} bludgeoning damage.

description.p: You count as one size larger when determining your carrying capacity and the weight you can push, drag, or lift.

sheet.description: You count as one size larger when determining carrying capacity and the weight you can push, drag, or lift.

description.p: Immediately after hitting a creature with your bite attack, you can make a grapple attempt against that creature with advantage without using an action.

sheet.description: After hitting with your bite, you can make a grapple attempt with advantage without using an action.
